body_txt: In some works of fiction, prostitution is portrayed as a dream job, or at least as a pleasant and reasonably unproblematic career, even if the sex worker would like to move on to another occupation someday. Common elements include: If a character in this role has a personality of their own (rather than being a pure Ms. Fanservice or similar), don't be surprised if they're a really good person who you can really talk with . Indeed, sometimes talking is as far as it goes , and that's OK, too. Another take on this trope could be that while yes, prostitution is traumatizing and coercive, it isn't uniquely so. Any form of menial labor has similar issues in their fields, and sex work is no worse or better. Sometimes Truth in Television in regard to #1, #2 and #5, since the girls would try to avoid at all costs people who look dangerous, violent, gross, visibly drunk or drugged, and respond to people who look nice, for their own safety note Seriously, when asked by reporters which is the quickest way to Squick them, it turned out to be guys who do drugs in their presence . Where prostitution is legal and regulated, #7 may be half-true for at least some—some might stiff her, but since she can sue them for her fee, this happens less often. Some forms of sex work, such as streaming video for an audience, are also inherently safer than one-on-one interactions with clients. In many depictions (and Truth in Television examples), the closer the prostitute is to working as a High-Class Call Girl or some equivalent thereof, the more it is possible (although far from certain) for this trope to be achieved. This is generally because, sex workers near the top of the totem pole (as opposed to a lowly streetwalker) is in theory able to earn more money for doing less work overall, which in turn allows for more education, greater freedom to pick and choose clients, greater control over their own earnings, and so forth. Contrast Secret Sex Worker , as the reason why the character needs to keep their work secret is because it's being problematized in-universe and Sex Work Karma , where sex work is treated as a punishment for evil deeds. Also see Platonic Prostitution , when a sex worker gets hired to do work that isn’t sexual. noreallife
